query: advantages of acid etching in dentistry

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Technique or Treatment -- Disadvantages
retrieved_docs.content: Air polishers are generally used on supragingival surfaces. Still, glycine powder air polishers are used for removing sub-gingival biofilm, resulting in less erosion of the soft tissues and an 80% reduction in abrasion of the root surface as compared to hand instrumentation or sodium bicarbonate air polishing. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Technique or Treatment -- Advantages
retrieved_docs.content: The main advantage of air-powder polishers is their ability to efficiently remove biofilm without harming the periodontal soft tissues or hard tissue structures. This method is faster and provides more patient comfort. It saves time, and hence, it can prevent patient and operator fatigue. It can be used on orthodontically bracketed teeth as it does not physically damage or disturb the bands, wires, or bonding cement. It reduces dentinal sensitivity by blocking the tubular opening with bicarbonate crystals. It reaches inaccessible areas that cannot be reached by rotary devices. [17]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Advantages and Disadvantages -- Bioceramic Sealers
retrieved_docs.content: Advantages: Biocompatible Ability to promote biomineralization Enhanced bonding between dentin and core material Dimensional stability upon setting [21] [22] Disadvantages: Technically difficult to manipulate Expensive Shorter shelf-life (cv other sealers) [21] [22]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Advantages and Disadvantages -- Resin Sealers
retrieved_docs.content: Advantages: Improved adherence Enhanced tolerability of apical tissues [19] [20] Disadvantages: Technically difficult to prepare deep aspects of root canal (potentially limits dentin-bonding ability) Difficult to remove in cases of endodontic retreatment Potential for allergic and mutagenic bodily responses Shrinkage upon setting [19] [20]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Advantages and Disadvantages -- Glass Ionomer Sealers
retrieved_docs.content: Advantages: Improved adherence (via dentin bonding) Increased resistance to root fracture Enhanced resistance to solubility in tissue fluids Antimicrobial properties Sustained fluoride release [16] [17] Disadvantages: Technically difficult to prepare deep aspects of root canal (potentially limits dentin-bonding ability) Difficult to remove in cases of endodontic retreatment [16] [17]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Advantages and Disadvantages -- Gutta Percha
retrieved_docs.content: Advantages: Ease of manipulation Radiopaque Low toxicity and plasticity Relatively inexpensive [1] [5] [25] Disadvantages: No dentin-bonding properties Material shrinkage upon setting [1] [5] [25] Bioceramic Core Obturation Materials Advantages: Biocompatible Ability to promote biomineralization High fracture resistance Antimicrobial Dimensional stability upon setting [26] [27] Disadvantages: Technically difficult to manipulate Expensive Shorter shelf-life (cv other core materials) [26] [27]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Advantages and Disadvantages -- Calcium Hydroxide Sealers
retrieved_docs.content: Advantages Antimicrobial properties Milder cytotoxicity Promotes tissue healing [13] Disadvantages Solubility

retrieved_docs.title: Pit and Fissure Sealants -- Technique or Treatment -- Acid Etching
retrieved_docs.content: The most frequently used agent for acid etching is 37% orthophosphoric acid. The gel can be applied directly with special application tips or with a small disposable brush. Application should cover all susceptible pits and fissures, extending up to the cuspal inclines. The following steps are typical techniques used for etching: Etch permanent molars for 15 seconds and primary teeth for 15 to 30 seconds. Teeth affected by dental fluorosis require additional etching time. When glass ionomer cement is used, etching is not required; instead, apply a surface conditioner such as polyacrylic acid. Rinse thoroughly with an air-water spray. Dry the tooth with uncontaminated compressed air until a frosty white opaque appearance becomes visible. Replace cotton rolls if cotton roll isolation has been used. Re-etch the surface if contamination occurs. [22]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Advantages and Disadvantages -- ZOE Sealers
retrieved_docs.content: Advantages: Absorption, if extruded past the apex Anti-inflammatory effects Antimicrobial effects [5] [9] Disadvantages: Transition into a weak, porous state upon setting Shrinkage upon setting [5] [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Disadvantages
retrieved_docs.content: Poor mechanical properties, eg, low compressive strength, low abrasion resistance, and fracture resistance, make its use restricted to low stress-bearing areas [5] [7] Poor esthetics due to lack of translucency Moisture sensitive while setting [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Pit and Fissure Sealants -- Equipment -- Glass ionomer sealants
retrieved_docs.content: Glass ionomer sealants provide a valuable alternative when resin placement is contraindicated, eg, in partially erupted molars or areas with poor isolation. These materials release fluoride over time, supporting long-term caries prevention. [18] [19] Conventional glass ionomers set through an acid-base reaction between fluoroaluminosilicate glass and polyacrylic acid, while resin-modified glass ionomers incorporate monomers, eg, 2-hydroxyethyl methacrylate (HEMA) or urethane dimethacrylate (UDMA), undergoing both acid-base and light-initiated polymerization to improve physical properties. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Properties -- Glass Ionomer Sealers
retrieved_docs.content: Clinicians advocating using glass ionomer sealers as an obturation material do so due to their dentin-bonding properties. [15] Dentin bonding results from the reaction between the polyacrylic acid in the glass ionomer and the hydroxyapatite on the dentinal wall and allows for excellent adhesion between the obturation material and the prepared canal wall. [16] Several major advantages arise from the improved adherence, including superior apical sealing, increased resistance to root fracture, and resistance to solubility in tissue fluids. [16] [17] Additional advantages include antimicrobial properties and sustained fluoride release. However, the dentinal walls are hard to reach with primer solutions in the middle and apical thirds, potentially limiting the adhesion of the sealer to the walls. [17] [18] Furthermore, the dentin-bonded glass ionomer sealer creates a homogenous solid material that is difficult to remove during endodontic retreatment. [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Pit and Fissure Sealants -- Equipment -- Hybrid sealants
retrieved_docs.content: Hybrid sealants represent a newer category and include materials such as compomers (polyacid-modified composite resins) and giomers. Compomers combine elements of both composites and glass ionomers, including reactive fillers and photo-initiators, while giomers contain pre-reacted glass ionomer (PRG) particles within a resin matrix. [20] Although these materials aim to merge the benefits of both parent systems, they are not yet widely used in clinical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: The selective infiltration etching (SIE) technique also can be used to increase the bond strength of zirconia and resin cement. A glass infiltration agent is heated and is allowed to penetrate the void space between the zirconia grains to re-arrange them. Then the glass infiltration agent is removed using hydrofluoric acid, resulting in the development of inter-grain nano-porosity and generating a relatively rough and reactive surface that exhibits enhanced micro-mechanical bond strength with resin cement.

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: Zirconia does not contain silica and thus hydrofluoric acid etching cannot be used. Therefore, other methods of surface treatments are used for zirconia such as grinding/polishing, surface coating, sandblasting, primer treatment, and laser treatment. Sandblasting with aluminum oxide particles results in irregularities and shallow pits in the surface of zirconia, which improves the micromechanical retention. In addition, coating with a 10-methacryloyloxydecyl dihydrogen phosphate (MDP)-containing silane primer after sandblasting can enhance chemical and micro-mechanical bond strength.

retrieved_docs.title: Teeth Polishing -- Technique or Treatment -- Disadvantages
retrieved_docs.content: Since the slurry uses sodium bicarbonate, it should be used cautiously in patients with restricted sodium diets. In such cases, non-sodium prophy powder, which contains aluminum trihydroxide, can be used. It should be carefully used in patients with respiratory, renal, or metabolic disease, diuretics, long-term steroid therapy, those having titanium implants, or with children and in patients with infectious diseases. Infection control is a problem with these devices since they produce aerosols. Hence, pretreatment washes are recommended. [17] There is a risk of subcutaneous emphysema intraorally. It is important to follow the manufacturer's instructions to avoid such complications. [7]

retrieved_docs.content: Antacida are classified within the broader domain of pharmacological effects – molecular mechanisms, indicating that their therapeutic action is understood at the level of molecular pharmacology. Within this class, aluminum hydroxide (hydroxid hlinitý) is a specific antacid agent, representing a concrete example of the antacid category. This relationship highlights that antacids, including aluminum hydroxide, are studied and described in terms of their molecular mechanisms of action.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 2288
retrieved_docs.content: Antacids are agents that neutralize gastric acid and are classified within a broader pharmacological parent category that describes drug actions at the molecular level. In clinical practice, antacids are considered contraindicated when used together with famotidin, an H₂‑receptor antagonist, because concurrent administration can interfere with famotidin’s ability to reduce acid secretion and may lead to reduced therapeutic effectiveness or adverse interactions. Understanding that antacids belong to a parent group of molecular‑mechanism‑based drug effects, while also recognizing their contraindication with famotidin, helps clinicians select appropriate acid‑reducing therapies and avoid potential drug‑interaction pitfalls.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2039
retrieved_docs.content: Iron in other combinations is classified as an iron antianemic preparation. Iron antianemic preparations are therapeutic agents used to replenish iron stores and treat iron‑deficiency anemia. Formulations described as “iron in other combinations” contain iron together with additional compounds or nutrients, enhancing absorption or providing complementary benefits while still serving the primary purpose of correcting iron deficiency. Recognizing these combination products as a subset of iron antianemic preparations helps clinicians select appropriate therapy for patients with anemia, especially when co‑existing deficiencies or specific tolerability issues are present.
